what_input_feature: A larger (~110-122 byte) input carrying a stable, well-formed header structure (the 0x06-0x1f 'pp@3&8Mnn' run plus a 'Gte;' / 'TE' token region) that sets data->set.http_transfer_encoding while NOT supplying a user 'TE' header, so the && at 3051 takes the false side and enters the TE block.
why_winner_satisfies: All three winner-resolving seeds are 110-122 bytes, share the identical structured prefix run '00 01 00 00 00 19 70 70 40 33 26 38 4d 6e 6e' and contain '47 74 65' ('Gte') near offset 0x24, exercising the false branch (T=0 F=3 at L3051); the minimizer's calibration burst awards more havoc energy to these faster/smaller/stable corpus entries, letting it preserve and extend the structured prefix.
why_loser_doesnt: All naive loser-blocking seeds are only 35-36 bytes and degenerate into '%'/'A'/'-' filler with no 'Gte'/'TE' token region; they take the true branch (T=15 F=0 at L3051), so naive never assembles the longer structured input that toggles http_transfer_encoding without a TE header.
mechanism_attribution: minimizer differs from naive only by the CalibrationStage + StdPowerMutationalStage (calibrated energy): perf_score derived from execution time/edge-fill/stability gives more havoc mutations to the structured corpus entries, so the same havoc/token mutators reach and stabilize the longer TE-enabling input that plain naive's flat StdMutationalStage budget does not.

what_input_feature: The same GPOS-attachment-flag-setting font; here the winning edge is reaching it faster by spending many more mutations on the small, stable kern/GPOS seeds that already approach the structure.
why_winner_satisfies: minimizer resolves 9/10 with the highest hitcount (208) and the shortest blocked duration (7.30h); its calibration-derived power schedule grants more havoc mutations to faster/smaller/more-stable seeds, concentrating effort on the compact font seeds that are near the attachment-triggering structure.
why_loser_doesnt: naive blocks 8/10 (15 hits, 21.80h) because its plain StdMutationalStage spends uniform energy per seed and never amplifies the promising compact font seeds, so it explores the structural neighborhood far more slowly.
mechanism_attribution: minimizer's calibrated energy (CalibrationStage perf_score driving a StdPowerMutationalStage with no rarity weighting) reallocates mutation budget toward the stable seeds nearest the GPOS structure, beating naive's flat scheduling.

why_loser_doesnt: naive runs a plain StdMutationalStage with no calibrated energy, blocks 8/10, and stays at T=0 F=180 on line 124; its loser seeds are unstructured fragments that the uniform mutation budget never refines into a valid shaping input.
mechanism_attribution: minimizer's CalibrationStage + StdPowerMutationalStage (calibrated energy) concentrates mutation effort on small stable valid-font seeds, accelerating discovery of an input that shapes a buffer with an UNSAFE_TO_BREAK glyph; naive's flat energy schedule does not.

what_input_feature: An input decoded to HB_SCRIPT_KHUDAWADI whose current glyph is codepoint 0x112B0u and whose NEXT glyph codepoint (buffer->cur(1)) is one of the inner-switch arm values 0x112E0u/0x112E5u/0x112E6u/0x112E7u/0x112E8u, so the false (non-default) branch of the codepoint switch at line 386 is taken and matched=true.
why_winner_satisfies: The minimizer winning-resolving seeds carry the 4-byte little-endian KHUDAWADI codepoints in sequence: bytes `b0 12 01 00` (=0x000112B0) immediately followed by `e5 12 01 00` (=0x000112E5), repeated across the buffer; the winner reaches the inner case arms (line 388-390 marked [W]) and takes the false branch (W F=4 at line 386).
why_loser_doesnt: The naive loser-blocking seeds repeat `b0 12 01 00` (0x112B0) but pair it with non-matching followers (e.g. `ff 00 00 00`, `40 00 00 00`), so at the line-386 switch they take only the true/default-fall-through side (L T=25 F=0) and never reach the inner arms; the winning false branch is taken 0 times.
mechanism_attribution: Both fuzzers share naive's edge counters and the identical havoc/token mutator stack, so the only technique delta is minimizer's CalibrationStage + StdPowerMutationalStage: faster/smaller/more-stable seeds earn more havoc mutations (energy from calibration perf_score, PowerSchedule None). This higher per-seed mutation budget on the well-behaved KHUDAWADI seeds lets minimizer stumble onto the specific 4-byte follower codepoint that naive's flat StdMutationalStage budget rarely reaches within the budget.

source_line: case OP_Lt:               /* same as TK_LT, jump, in1, in3 */
what_input_feature: Same program-side requirement: a parseable SELECT containing a '<' operator (literal 0x3c) so the compiled OP_Lt arm is dispatched. No CMP introspection is available; the feature must be reached by more havoc mutations spent on promising seeds.
why_winner_satisfies: The minimizer winning seeds 4 and 5 contain a literal '<' (0x3c): seed 4 is '53 45 4c 45 43 54 20 31 31 31 31 30 3c 31 31 2a' decoding to 'SELECT 11110<11*', an explicit '<' comparison that compiles to OP_Lt; minimizer resolves 9/10 with avg hitcount 568 on the branch.
why_loser_doesnt: Naive (same mutators, plain StdMutationalStage, no calibrated energy) blocks 8/10 and reaches the branch True side 0 times; its loser-blocking seeds contain neither a parseable SELECT nor a '<' operator, and naive spends uniform energy so it never lucks into the long valid '...<...' expression.
mechanism_attribution: minimizer runs havoc+token in a StdPowerMutationalStage whose per-seed mutation count (energy) is derived from a calibration-based perf_score (faster/smaller/more-stable seeds earn more mutations). The extra calibrated mutation budget on parseable seeds raises the odds of havoc producing the valid 'SELECT ... < ...' byte sequence that compiles to OP_Lt — purely a scheduling effect, no CMP introspection.

what_input_feature: A SQL statement that parses far enough to call resolveExprStep on an expression tree AND raises a name/function resolution error so pParse->nErr becomes non-zero by line 98693 (e.g. an arithmetic SELECT over unresolved identifiers or a 'no such function' call), driving the ternary's true (WRC_Abort) side.
why_winner_satisfies: The winner seed is a 482-byte 'SELECT   - 11111+++1++1,...' arithmetic expression; minimizer resolves the branch 8/10 with avg hitcount 4 and the true side observed (W shows T=1 at L98693), consistent with a deeply-walked expression that accumulates a resolver error.
why_loser_doesnt: naive resolves only 1/10 (blocked 9), avg hitcount 0, and W/L counts show the loser only ever takes the false side (T=0 F=1870 at L98693); naive's plain mutation budget rarely produces the long well-formed expression needed to both reach the walk and set nErr.
mechanism_attribution: minimizer adds a CalibrationStage + StdPowerMutationalStage so faster/smaller/more-stable seeds earn more havoc mutations (calibrated energy); this concentrates mutation budget on the SELECT-expression seeds that walk deep enough to set pParse->nErr, whereas naive's flat StdMutationalStage spreads the same havoc operators thinly and rarely lands the error-raising expression.
